records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Disfranchised (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Disfranchising.]

defs: To deprive of a franchise or chartered right; to dispossess of the rights of a citizen, or of a particular privilege, as of voting, holding office, etc.
quotes: He was partially disfranchised so as to be made incapable of taking part in public affairs.
